name: Public Bath
calculationsAnalysis: The Public Bath balances basic health infrastructure with modest service fees. The construction cost of 2,000,000 ducats reflects the specialized water and heating systems required, while the income of 15,000 ducats (0.75% return) represents modest usage fees. The maintenance cost of 17,500 ducats exceeds income, creating a realistic scenario where public hygiene facilities required subsidy but provided essential health benefits. The employment capacity of 10 represents the attendants and maintenance staff needed. The construction time of 5 days (432000000ms) reflects the specialized construction requirements. This building creates an accessible investment in public health that improves population wellbeing and disease resistance, reflecting Venice's historically advanced approach to public hygiene compared to other European cities of the era.
shortDescription: Hygiene facility providing bathing services to urban residents without private facilities.
fullDescription: Supporting public health through cleanliness, bath houses provide washing facilities for citizens without private baths. Featuring bathing pools of varying temperatures, steam rooms, changing areas, and often medicinal services, these establishments combine practical hygiene with social gathering. Venice's water-surrounded situation made public baths particularly important for controlling disease and maintaining cleanliness.
flavorText: Cleanliness of body promotes health of body, and the shared conversation of the baths promotes health of mind—thus a city with good bath houses benefits twice over.

locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Improves public health, creates social gathering point

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian public bath under construction, community hygiene facility, bathing pools of different sizes being installed with waterproofing, heating system with furnace and water pipes, steam room with specialized ventilation, changing areas with privacy considerations, separate sections for men and women, water supply and drainage systems being carefully engineered, medicinal herb steeping area, massage tables, resting benches, maintenance access for cleaning, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on practical public health infrastructure with social amenities
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian public bath, community hygiene facility featuring multiple bathing pools at different temperatures, steam room with aromatic herbs, separate sections for men and women with appropriate modesty features, changing areas with benches and clothing storage, attendants providing towels and assistance, heating system keeping water warm, herb-infused water for medicinal properties, massage area for additional services, resting benches for relaxation after bathing, small refreshment area, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, atmosphere of cleanliness and rejuvenation, social interaction among patrons, balance of practical hygiene and community gathering, steam rising from warm pools, smooth stone surfaces showing quality construction focused on cleanliness

upgradePath: Luxury Bath House (enhanced services, higher class)
